report_name: Five Eyes Nations Warn Frontier AI Models Will Reshape Cybersecurity 'Within Months'

threat_actors.name: AI-Powered Threat Actors
threat_actors.description: A future class of threat actors, ranging from individuals to nation-states, who will leverage publicly available frontier AI models to conduct advanced cyberattacks at scale, as warned by the Five Eyes intelligence alliance.
threat_actors.goals: To conduct automated vulnerability discovery, sophisticated social engineering, and deploy adaptive malware for various malicious purposes including espionage, disruption, and financial gain.

attack_patterns.name: Active Scanning
attack_patterns.description: Future AI models are predicted to be used for automated vulnerability discovery, enabling threat actors to find and exploit weaknesses at machine speed.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Frontier AI models are expected to enable the creation of hyper-realistic phishing campaigns, significantly increasing the effectiveness of social engineering attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: The advisory warns of AI-powered malware that can dynamically alter its own code to evade detection, a form of advanced obfuscation.

tools.name: Fable 5
tools.description: A frontier AI model from Anthropic, cited in the Five Eyes warning as an example of a system whose capabilities could soon be used for malicious cyber operations.

tools.name: Daybreak
tools.description: A frontier AI model from OpenAI, cited in the Five Eyes warning as an example of a system whose capabilities could soon be used for malicious cyber operations.

tools.name: Frontier AI Models
tools.description: A class of advanced Artificial Intelligence models predicted by the Five Eyes alliance to become broadly accessible and capable of creating significant cyber threats.

identities.name: Five Eyes
identities.description: An intelligence alliance comprising the U.S., U.K., Canada, Australia, and New Zealand that issued a joint statement warning of imminent AI-powered cyber threats.

identities.name: National Security Agency (NSA)
identities.description: A U.S. intelligence agency that co-signed the joint statement on the threat of AI-powered hacking models.

identities.name: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA)
identities.description: A U.S. cybersecurity agency that co-signed the joint statement on the threat of AI-powered hacking models.

identities.name: Anthropic
identities.description: An AI company mentioned as the developer of the 'Fable 5' frontier model.

identities.name: OpenAI
identities.description: An AI company mentioned as the developer of the 'Daybreak' frontier model.

identities.name: David Imbordino
identities.description: NSA Director of Cybersecurity and co-signer of the joint statement.

identities.name: Nick Andersen
identities.description: Acting CISA Director and co-signer of the joint statement.

identities.name: Critical Infrastructure
identities.description: A sector identified as a high-value target for future sophisticated AI-powered attacks.

identities.name: Government and Defense Organizations
identities.description: A sector identified as being directly threatened by AI-powered espionage, disruption, and attacks.

identities.name: Technology and AI Companies
identities.description: A sector identified as being both creators of advanced AI and prime targets for its misuse.

identities.name: Cybersecurity Providers
identities.description: A sector whose defensive technology stack must evolve rapidly to counter AI-powered threats.
